chunks.title: Catalog Description
chunks.content: Operations Research II. (3-0). Credit 3. Probabilistic methods for industrial and service systems; stochastic processes used in industrial engineering, including Poisson processes and discrete and continuous-time Markov chains; applications to production operations, inventory control, revenue management, quality control, reliability, digital simulation and finance. Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MATH 304 or MATH 323; grade of C or better in ISEN 310, STAT 212, or ECEN 303; junior or senior classification.

chunks.title: Course Prerequisites
chunks.content: Prerequisite/Corequisite(s): Grade of C or better in MATH 304 or MATH 323; grade of C or better in ISEN 310, STAT 212, or ECEN 303; junior or senior classification.

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
10% Pre-Lecture Quizzes*
15% Post-Lecture Quizzes and Assignments*
25% Exam 1
25% Exam 2
25% Exam 3

* Can miss or drop 2; no make ups.

[90%,100%] gets an A, [80%,90%) gets a B, [70%,80%) gets a C, [60%, 70%) gets a D, and less than 60% gets an F.

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content: Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

chunks.title: Course Specific Late Work Policy
chunks.content: Late work will not be accepted.

chunks.title: Scientific Software
chunks.content: MATLAB, R, Excel, or Python might also be used for classwork and homework.

chunks.title: Course Format
chunks.content:
• Traditional lectures with in-class exercises, quizzes, and evaluations.
• Read the provided handouts, including examples, and try to solve the exercises in the handouts.
• Come to class and make an effort to understand each topic.
• Review the class exercises; optionally, supplement your preparation by working through the suggested textbooks.
• Use the TA's and the instructor's office hours.
• Turn in quizzes; if anything is unclear, make a note of each. Partial credit will be given.

chunks.title: Grade Contest Policy
chunks.content:
• To contest a grade, please submit a formal written objection within three days of the graded evaluation's due date.
• If necessary, tests and exams might be regraded to ensure the integrity and accuracy of the questions. Some students' grades may decrease after the regrading.

chunks.title: Communications
chunks.content: To maintain course integrity, all communications must be conducted through Canvas (via a message). The instructor will not respond to course-related questions via email or chat.

chunks.title: How to Succeed in the Course?
chunks.content:
• Read and abide by the Aggie Code of Honor
• Be respectful of your peers and instructor.
• Plan to spend 9 hours on the course each week, including 3 hours of class attendance.
• Do not put off tasks to the last minute; this course requires extreme diligence.
• Ask questions during the course meetings.
• Solve the exercise problems independently before consulting the solution.
• If something is unclear, ask questions in class or during office hours (seek help as early as possible).
• Attempt all the exercise problems before coming to class.
• Prepare extremely well for quizzes and exams.
• This is a math-based class, so the procedure to obtain an answer is as valid as the answer itself.

chunks.title: AI Statement
chunks.content: AI should be used if the instructor indicates it should.
